paper.title_en: Josephson Arbitrary Waveform Synthesizer for Flux-Ramp Modulation in Microwave SQUID Multiplexers
paper.abstract_en: Microwave superconducting quantum interference device (SQUID) multiplexers (µMUX) have become a key technology for the readout of large arrays of low-temperature detectors, particularly in cosmic microwave background experiments. A critical element of this technique is the flux-ramp modulation signal, whose noise, especially at low frequencies, directly limits the readout performance. This work proposes and experimentally demonstrates the use of a Josephson arbitrary waveform synthesizer (JAWS) as a low-noise, high-precision source for generating the sawtooth-shaped flux-ramp signal required in µMUX readout systems. The JAWS, based on the AC Josephson effect, provides intrinsically quantized and spectrally pure waveforms with excellent signal-to-noise ratio. The operation of a 9000-junction JAWS array is characterized and integrated with a cryogenic µMUX device and a custom software defined radio (SDR)-based readout system. With this setup, the noise floor level around the harmonic typically used for phase demodulation of the SQUID signal to recover the detector signal was <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$-104 \,\mathrm{dB}{\mathrm{c}} / \mathrm{Hz}$</tex-math></inline-formula>, and the signal-to-noise ratio of this harmonic was <inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"><tex-math notation="LaTeX">$73 \,\mathrm{dB}$</tex-math></inline-formula>. These results demonstrate the feasibility of using JAWS-based waveform synthesis for µMUX flux-ramp generation.
